Defendants GORDON PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, STEPHANIE GORDON, and HAZEN FAMILY TRUST, by and through its Successor Trustee TIMOTHY ALLEN HAZEN'S Motion to Strike Complaint is Granted with leave to amend to allege facts re: defendants' malice, oppression or fraud. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on March 14, 2014. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
Defendants GORDON PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, STEPHANIE GORDON, and HAZEN FAMILY TRUST, by and through its Successor Trustee TIMOTHY ALLEN HAZEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for (1) breach of contract to allege landlord's obligation under a contract and breach of such obligation and to allege facts re" landlord's failure substantially comply with applicable building and housing code standards which materially affect health and safety ; (2) wrongful eviction, unlawful actions by landlord and unlawful business practices to allege facts re: series of acts designed to deprive tenant of beneficial enjoyment of the premises; (3) retaliation to allege facts showing that landlords' motive for not improving the soundproof and not enforcing neighbor's lease was retaliation. Demurer is overruled as to causes of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment, negligence and nuisance. Whether landlord had a duty to enforce the neighbor's lease or control neighbor's alleged behavior is an issue of fact. Davis v. Gomez (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 1401 is inapposite as it was decided on summary judgment. Prevailing party to submit a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (D501)
